title: Atractocarpus deplanchei
intro: Atractocarpus deplanchei, also known as the New Caledonian vine or Deplanche's vine, is a woody climber native to New Caledonia, characterized by its fragrant, white flowers and edible fruit. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Atractocarpus deplanchei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed for their perceived health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Atractocarpus deplanchei belongs to the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest families of flowering plants, with over 13,000 species. Atractocarpus deplanchei is a species of flowering plant that is native to New Caledonia. It is a member of the genus Atractocarpus, which consists of several species of plants that are primarily found in the Pacific Islands region.
